name: buttons

key_functions.button_process: Main button processing pipeline: raw OSContPad → 4-nibble LUT → analog→d-pad → remap → mirror → lock → final buttons field

summary: Controller input processing: remaps raw button bits via 4 nibble-based lookup tables (C-buttons, triggers, D-pad, face buttons), converts analog stick (±40 threshold) to digital d-pad, applies per-player remap table, mirror mode (swap L↔R), and lock/filter. Called from gfx_render_frame in main.c each frame.

functions.name: button_remap
functions.summary: Button remap via per-player lookup table gRemapTable[player][8]. For each of 8 low bits, if set, ORs in the remapped value. High byte (0xFF00) passes through unchanged. Called from button_process when remap enabled (x_3fb684fe).

functions.name: button_remap_reset
functions.summary: Reset remap table to identity: ptr[i] = 1 << i. Called from rsp_game_init in rsp.c for both players.

functions.name: button_process
functions.summary: Main button processing pipeline: reads raw OSContPad.button, decomposes into 4 nibbles (C-buttons/triggers/D-pad/face), looks up each in respective LUT (sCButtonLut/sTriggerLut/sDPadLut/sFaceLut). Converts analog stick (±40 threshold) to digital d-pad when analog enabled. Applies remap (button_remap), mirror mode (swap L↔R), sticky lock filter via x_33260da8/x_245d87f4. NON_MATCHING.

functions.name: button_update_frame
functions.summary: Per-frame button update: increments gFrameCounter, calls controller_read_frame, then processes buttons for each enabled+connected player via button_process. Zeros buttons for disabled players. Called from gfx_render_frame (main.c:123).

functions.name: button_state_reset
functions.summary: Reset both players' button state flags: clears sticky lock (x_c4397934), zeros buttons, disables mirror mode (x_d93bcabf), enables both players, resets previous-buttons (x_33260da8), enables remap (x_3fb684fe), disables analog→d-pad (x_40ccbb76). Called from rsp_game_init and rsp_game_reinit.

globals.sCButtonLut: C-button nibble lookup table (16 entries, indexed by low nibble): R/L/D/U_CBUTTONS
globals.sTriggerLut: Trigger nibble lookup table (8 entries, indexed by nibble 1): R/L_TRIG
globals.sDPadLut: D-pad nibble lookup table (11 entries, indexed by nibble 2): R/L/D/U_JPAD
globals.sFaceLut: Face button nibble lookup table (16 entries, indexed by nibble 3): START/Z/B/A
globals.gRemapTable[2][8]: Per-player button remap table — player can reassign what each of 8 physical buttons maps to (used by select.c option screen)

open_questions: Nibble decomposition of raw button value: the raw u16 from OSContPad is split into 4×4-bit nibbles. This suggests the controller data isn't in standard N64 OS_CONT format, or the game pre-processes it differently. How does the raw value get formed?

Player fields x_12a7bd86 (connected), x_40ccbb76 (analog disable), x_f3756179 (pre-filter buttons), x_3fb684fe (remap enable), x_d93bcabf (mirror), x_c4397934 (sticky lock), x_245d87f4 (lock mask), x_33260da8 (prev buttons) — all need meaningful names in Player struct (Wave 2).

LUT array sizes: sTriggerLut[8] has only 4 used entries (indices 1-3); sDPadLut[11] has 10 used entries (indices 1-10); sFaceLut[16] has 7 used entries (indices 1,2,4,6,8,10,12). All have trailing zeros — leftover space or intentional padding?
